# SECTION 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics

## 4.1 Cost-Volume-Profit (CVP) Block Architecture

The following **block diagram** illustrates the fundamental architecture of Break Even Analysis, mapping how each component (Cost, Volume, Profit) interacts within an agro-industrial enterprise.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    A[AGRO INDUSTRIAL UNIT]
    A --> B[FIXED COST BLOCK FC]
    A --> C[VARIABLE COST BLOCK VC]
    B --> B1[Plant Rent]
    B --> B2[Depreciation of Machinery]
    B --> B3[Permanent Staff Salary]
    B --> B4[Insurance and Loan Interest]
    C --> C1[Raw Agricultural Produce]
    C --> C2[Packaging Materials]
    C --> C3[Casual Labour and Fuel]
    B --> D[TOTAL COST TC EQUALS FC PLUS VC times Q]
    C --> D
    D --> E[TOTAL REVENUE TR EQUALS p times Q]
    E --> F{COMPARISON TR versus TC}
    F -->|TR greater than TC| G[PROFIT ZONE]
    F -->|TR equals TC| H[BREAK EVEN POINT]
    F -->|TR less than TC| I[LOSS ZONE]
    G --> J[CONTRIBUTION MARGIN CM times Q minus FC]
    H --> K[ZERO PROFIT ZERO LOSS]
    I --> L[OPERATING DEFICIT]
```

## 4.2 Break Even Chart (Block Schematic Representation)

The conventional **Break Even Chart** is a two-dimensional plot with **Sales/Output Volume** on the X-axis and **Cost/Revenue (₹)** on the Y-axis. Since a Mermaid physical plot is constrained, the following schematic represents the layered architecture of the chart with annotated intersection logic.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    subgraph BREAK_EVEN_CHART_ARCHITECTURE
        direction TB
        M1[Revenue Line TR STARTS AT ORIGIN SLOPE equal to selling price p]
        M2[Total Cost Line TC STARTS AT FC ON Y AXIS SLOPE equal to variable cost v]
        M3[Fixed Cost Line FC HORIZONTAL LINE AT LEVEL OF FIXED COST]
        M4[LOSS REGION AREA BELOW TR LINE AND ABOVE TC LINE LEFT OF BEP]
        M5[PROFIT REGION AREA BELOW TR LINE AND ABOVE TC LINE RIGHT OF BEP]
        M6[BREAK EVEN POINT INTERSECTION OF TR AND TC LINES]
        M7[BEP IN RUPEES HORIZONTAL PROJECTION TO Y AXIS]
        M8[BEP IN UNITS VERTICAL PROJECTION TO X AXIS]
    end
    M1 --> M6
    M2 --> M6
    M3 --> M2
    M6 --> M7
    M6 --> M8
```

## 4.3 Decision Logic Flow for BEA Application

```mermaid
flowchart TB
    S0[START Identify Project Parameters]
    S1[Classify Costs into Fixed and Variable]
    S2{Is Single Product or Multi Product}
    S2 -->|Single Product| S3[Compute CM equal to p minus v]
    S2 -->|Multi Product| S4[Determine Sales Mix Proportions w sub i]
    S3 --> S5[Compute BEP units equal to FC divided by CM]
    S4 --> S6[Compute Weighted Average CM equal to sum of w sub i times CM sub i]
    S6 --> S7[Compute BEP units equal to FC divided by Weighted CM]
    S5 --> S8[Compute BEP in Rupees equal to p times BEP units]
    S7 --> S8
    S8 --> S9[Compute Margin of Safety]
    S9 --> S10[Evaluate Project Viability and Risk]
    S10 --> S11[Decision ACCEPT or REJECT or MODIFY]
```

## 4.4 Conceptual Graph Mapping (ASCII Representation)

For students who wish to sketch the **Break Even Chart** in their KTU exam answer scripts, the following textual layout captures the key features:

```
   ₹ (Cost / Revenue)
    |
    |                                          TR (Sales Revenue Line)
    |                                         /
    |                                        /
 FC |-- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - /-
    |                                     /|
    |                                    / |
    |                            ______/  |
    |                          _/    ___  | <- PROFIT AREA
    |                       _/    _/   \_|
    |                     _/   _/  LOSS   \
    |                  _/   _/     AREA     \
    |               _/   _/                  \
    |            _/   _/                      \
    |         _/   _/                          \
    |      _/   _/                              \
    |   _/   _/                                  \
    | _/   _/                                      \
    |/_/______________________________________________\____________
    0        Q_BE                                  Q (Volume in units)
             (Break Even Quantity)
```

**Key Features of the Chart:**
- The **Total Cost (TC) line** starts at a positive intercept equal to **FC** on the Y-axis (because even at zero output, fixed costs exist).
- The **Total Revenue (TR) line** starts from the **origin (0, 0)** because no sales implies no revenue.
- The **slope of TR line** = $p$ (selling price per unit).
- The **slope of TC line** = $v$ (variable cost per unit).
- The **gap between TC line and the horizontal FC line** represents Total Variable Cost.
- The **intersection** of TR and TC defines the **Break Even Point**, with coordinates $(Q_{BE}, \text{BEP}_{₹})$.
- The **triangular area to the left of BEP** between TR and TC is the **Loss Area**.
- The **triangular area to the right of BEP** between TR and TC is the **Profit Area**.
